items.title: A guide to targeting with Sponsored Products
items.excerpt: Discover tips to help you drive sales through targeting with Sponsored Products.
items.body: Guide A simple guide to effective targeting with Sponsored Products Tips to help you drive sales through targeting with Sponsored Products. Table of contents 1 . An introduction to Sponsored Products targeting What is targeting? 2 . Targeting options with Sponsored Products Automatic targeting Manual targeting Negative targeting Deciding which targeting strategy 3 . Automatic targeting with Sponsored Products Remember this When to choose automatic targeting Just getting started with Sponsored Products? Already have active manual campaigns? 4 . Manual targeting (keywords) with Sponsored Products More about match types Remember this Top tips Keyword types Do your research To note, for Kindle Direct Publishing (KDP) authors and book vendors Choose keyword targeting when: 5 . Manual targeting (product) with Sponsored Products Remember this Choose product targeting when: 6 . Negative targeting with Sponsored Products Consider adding the following as negative keywords Remember this 7 . Setting up strategic targeting for Sponsored Products campaigns How to be strategic Start by defining your business goals Next, choose your campaign strategy 6 tips to remember How campaign structure can impact advertising performance 8 . Sponsored Products targeting checklist An introduction to Sponsored Products targeting Strong targeting is crucial to build a solid foundation for your Sponsored Products campaigns, and can help to match shoppers intents to your products. What is targeting? Targeting is the way you define the context in which you want your ads to appear. That can be done in multiple ways. The most common is the use of keywords that match your ad with the shopping queries customers use when looking for something online. This guide will explore everything you need to know about...

items.title: SP-API Release Notes
items.excerpt: Selling Partner API new features, software updates, and changelog.
items.body: For releases prior to January 19, 2022, refer to Announcements in the Selling Partner API GitHub repo . May 27, 2026 API changes Listings Restrictions API v2021-08-01 Added the optional productType query parameter to the getListingsRestrictions operation. You can use this parameter along with a brand name to evaluate GTIN exemption restrictions for a specific product type. Notifications API v1 Added the filterExpression field to the createSubscription operation. You can use this field to filter events before notification delivery. Filters are applied to the notification payload using Common Expression Language (CEL) syntax. Added DeliveryTipChange as a supported order change type to the OrderChangeType notification schema. You can use this to track the status of a delivery tip for an order. Orders API v0 The following updates apply to veterinary products: Added the approvedAlternativeDetails field to the VerificationDetails object in the updateVerificationStatus operation. You can use this field to submit pre-approved alternative product attributes. Added the interimStatusDetail field to the VerificationDetails object in the updateVerificationStatus operation. You can use this field to provide granular progress updates while an order is pending verification to indicate that a veterinarian has been contacted. Added the ValidInterimStatusCodes field to the RegulatedOrderVerificationStatus object in the getOrderRegulatedInfo response. This field returns the list of supported interim status codes and customer-facing descriptions that you can use when you provide an interimStatusDetail . Orders API v2026-01-01 Added the breakdowns array to the getOrder and searchOrders operations. You can use this attribute to show an order's proceeds breakdown, including delivery tip...

The disc...
items.body: Amazon PPC can drain a budget fast when sellers rely on the wrong keywords, match types, or Q4 strategy. In this Amazon PPC Masterclass sneak peek, the My Amazon Guy team explains why set-and-forget campaigns fail, how sellers should use real search term data before making decisions, and why Black Friday, Cyber Monday, and other deal events can raise CPCs and hurt results when a product has no strong offer. The discussion also covers keyword relevance, exact match risks, audience targeting, B2B ads, Brand Analytics, Data Dive, Helium 10, and Jungle Scout. Ready to improve Amazon PPC performance and cut wasted ad spend? Book a call with My Amazon Guy: https://bit.ly/4jMZtxu #AmazonPPC #AmazonAdvertising #AmazonFBA #AmazonSeller #PPCStrategy Want free resources? items.title: # 运营案例分析 # 跑一个月广告，为什么手动广告能出单，而自动不能？产品是标品，主图和同行区别不大，还能做什么优化呢？请前辈指点
items.excerpt: # 请教各位前辈指点 1. 为什么手动广告能出单，而自动广告不能呢？ 2. 自动广告以前只开了紧密和广泛，大概三周时间，只有曝光，没有任何转化，就关了。后来尝试跑其他三个类型，但目前情况还是没有任何好转。 3. 在我的理解中，手动广告能出单，证明 listing 埋词应该没有问题。（不知道这个观点对不对，请前辈们多多指教。） 4. 价格上有微弱优势，比竞品少 0.2 欧左右。竞品评论有 50 个左右，我的产品目前有 14 个。最近在做...

items.title: 亚马逊AI图片合规新规实操分享 哪些图要打标怎么打标一次说清楚
items.excerpt: 最近亚马逊AI图片合规新规落地了，我踩了几个坑才搞清楚到底怎么操作，今天把完整流程整理出来分享给大家。 先说结论：凡是图片里包含AI生成的逼真人物，上传前必须在文件元数据里加上contains-synthetic-performer这个标签，不然Listing会被抑制显示甚至下架。不是不能用AI，而是要如实披露。 哪些图必须打标。完全由AI生成的真人模特穿戴产品图，真人照片经AI换脸或实质性修改面部体型，产品实拍抠图置入AI生成的虚拟...

How it Works Step 1: Set Rank Conditions Step 2: Set Bid Actions Step 3: Save and Automate Understanding Rank Measurement Speed of Setup Achieve More Results in Less Time With Helium 10 Sign Up For Free Find Your Next Best Performing Keywords Get a free skill that surfaces terms worth going after, with branded keywords pulled into their own list so you can decide whether to target them in PPC or leave them alone. Download Free Claude Skill Your keyword ranking data is one of your most valuable assets. It tells you exactly where you stand against the competition but lives separately from your bid strategy. Your ranking data should inform your bidding decisions, not sit in isolation. That’s where the Keyword Tracker Bid Rule comes in. It eliminates the manual work of adjusting bids by automating them based on your real-time ranking performance. Instead of juggling data between two tools, your bid strategy now responds directly to where you rank, helping you maintain visibility without overspending on ads. What if your ranking insights could automatically inform your bidding decisions? What is Keyword Tracker? Keyword Tracker continuously monitors your keyword positions in Amazon search results by checking your ASINs and keywords. Keyword rankings update daily by d...

items.title: # 运营案例分析 # 上架三个月后接手重推，售价14.99，CPC1.5-2.0，基本靠广告出单，每天2-3单还不稳定，除了广告还有其他方式能推起来吗？
items.excerpt: 前情条件： 入职新公司开荒，这条链接1月份上架，4月份之前都是上个运营在推，我接手后先开始优化链接和A+图片等，然后把广告架构也重新铺设（走的“农村包围城市”路线）。前期测试比较保守，推了一个多月后，每天基本能出单1-2单左右，都是广告单。后面因为货不够到六月底才接上，到货之后重新开推至今。（汗颜~TAT） 链接现状： 1. 产品售价14.99，加上5%的优惠券，评论数21条，评分4.7分； 2. 价格在市场均价左右，评论数量远低于头...

items.title: #757 -Inside A 9-Figure K-Beauty Brand
items.excerpt: How did a home-based reseller become a global e-commerce leader for a 9-figure K-beauty brand? Discover her Amazon strategy, future-customer targeting, TikTok tactics, and smart AI tools.
items.body: How did a home-based reseller become a global e-commerce leader for a 9-figure K-beauty brand? Discover her Amazon strategy, future-customer targeting, TikTok tactics, and smart AI tools. ► Watch The Podcasts On YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@Helium10SeriousSellersPodcast?sub_confirmation=1 ► Instagram: instagram.com/serioussellerspodcast ► Free Amazon Seller Chrome Extension: https://h10.me/extension ► Sign Up For Helium 10: https://h10.me/signup (Use SSP10 To Save 10% For Life) ► Learn How To Sell on Amazon: https://h10.me/ft Bradley Sutton takes the Serious Sellers Podcast on the road, and onto a moving rail bike in South Korea, for a conversation with Jenna, Global E-commerce Team Manager at TONYMOLY. Jenna shares how she went from studying law and working as a programmer to spending ten years raising her daughter, discovering e-commerce from home, and eventually building a successful international selling career. She began by reselling Korean sporting goods to US customers through eBay before shifting her attention to Amazon, where one business reached as much as $10,000 in monthly profit. Her success during the pandemic earned her recognition from Amazon Korea and opened the door to managing accounts for other companies. Jenna has now used Helium 10 for approximately five years, and her favorite tool is the Xray Chrome Extension. She uses Xray while browsing Amazon to quickly review estimated sales, keywords, and Best Sellers Rank, helping her evaluate products and marketplace opportunities without leaving the search results. Today, she applies that experience at TONYMOLY, a 20-year-old K-beauty company generating approximately $163 million in annual sales. Jenna also reveals an unusual long-term marketing strategy: targeting customers before they become the b...

items.title: #756 - Million $ Amazon Business Without Selling In The US?
items.excerpt: How did a European Amazon seller use AI, logistics, keyword data, and Helium 10 to scale millions without selling in the U.S.? Today’s guest reveals the hidden plays most sellers overlook.
items.body: How did a European Amazon seller use AI, logistics, keyword data, and Helium 10 to scale millions without selling in the U.S.? Today’s guest reveals the hidden plays most sellers overlook. ► Watch The Podcasts On YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@Helium10SeriousSellersPodcast?sub_confirmation=1 ► Instagram: instagram.com/serioussellerspodcast ► Free Amazon Seller Chrome Extension: https://h10.me/extension ► Sign Up For Helium 10: https://h10.me/signup (Use SSP10 To Save 10% For Life) ► Learn How To Sell on Amazon: https://h10.me/ft In this episode of the Serious Sellers Podcast, Bradley Sutton welcomes Bartłomiej Piątkowski, better known as Bart, an Amazon seller and agency operator from Poland who has sold millions in Europe and helped other brands do the same. What makes his story especially interesting is that he has built this success without selling his own products in the U.S. marketplace. Bart’s journey started far from the typical e-commerce path. Trained as a cook, he then worked in radio and electronics, and discovered Amazon in 2016 when his company was on the verge of shutting down. With only a small amount of money left, he began learning about Amazon through online communities, moved returned inventory from retail into Amazon Europe, and realized that marketplace margins could beat traditional retail margins. From there, his business expanded into private label brands, reselling, packaging products, textiles, and managing major brand relationships across Europe. The conversation gets tactical as Bart breaks down how his team uses Helium 10, AI, keyword data, and advertising automation to move faster. He explains how tools like Cerebro, Magnet, Search Query Performance, Helium 10 Ads, and the all-new Helium 10 MCP help his team understand how Amazon reads...
